---
title: "Workspaces & Notebooks"
subtitle: "Snowsight UX, compute, and what Workspace provides for R"
---

## Overview

Before bootstrapping R, understand **where** you work in Snowflake. **Snowsight** is the web UI. **Workspace** is the project-oriented development area within Snowsight. **Notebooks** are interactive, cell-based environments for Python, SQL, and (with this guide and package) R.

[Workspace Bootstrap](../06_workspace_bootstrap/index.qmd) covers `setup_notebook()` next.

::: {.callout-important}
**Workspace Notebooks required.** The multi-language R stack in this guide (`setup_notebook()`, `%%R`, snowflakeR) runs only in **Workspace Notebooks** on ML Container Runtime — not in legacy standalone Snowsight notebooks.
:::

## Learning Objectives

- Navigate Snowsight → Workspace → Notebook
- Choose and configure compute for a notebook session
- Use Python, SQL, and Markdown cells — and know where R fits
- Understand session lifecycle (start, active, restart, end)

---

## Snowsight and Workspace {#sec-snowsight}

**Snowsight** provides:

- SQL worksheets and query history
- Dashboards and Streamlit apps
- Admin and governance screens
- **Workspace** — projects, files, Git repos, notebooks

**Workspace** adds a developer workflow:

| Capability | Benefit |
|------------|---------|
| **Project + files** | Organize notebooks, scripts, configs (`snowflaker_config.yaml`) |
| **Git integration** | Clone, commit, push — team collaboration |
| **Notebooks** | Cell-based Python/SQL/Markdown (Jupyter-compatible in Workspaces) |
| **Compute session** | Attached warehouse + container runtime for kernel |

::: {.callout-note}
**Terminology:** "Workspace Notebook" means a notebook running **inside** your Snowflake account — not Jupyter on your laptop. Kernel and data stay in-account.
:::

### Screenshot placeholders {#sec-screenshots-snowsight}

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## TODO: Screenshot — Snowsight navigation

Capture: Left nav with **Projects & Workspace** (or equivalent) highlighted.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## TODO: Screenshot — Workspace landing

Capture: Project list with notebooks and Git repo indicator.
:::

## Workspace notebooks {#sec-workspace-notebooks}

All R content in this guide assumes **Notebooks in Workspaces** — the Jupyter-compatible experience with Git integration and ML Container Runtime. Create and open notebooks from **Snowsight → Workspace → project**.

## Creating and opening a notebook {#sec-create}

Typical flow:

1. Open **Snowsight** → **Workspace**
2. Select or create a **project** (optionally connect a Git repository)
3. **Create notebook** or open existing `.ipynb` / Snowflake notebook file
4. **Start session** — kernel comes online on container runtime
5. Set **execution context**: role, warehouse, database, schema ([docs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-snowsight/notebooks-in-workspaces/notebooks-in-workspaces-edit-run))
6. Run a SQL or Python smoke cell (`SELECT CURRENT_USER()`, etc.)

::: {.callout-warning}
Workspace does **not** auto-set database/schema in all versions — set context explicitly in YAML config or SQL, or fully qualify object names.
:::

### Screenshot placeholders {#sec-screenshots-create}

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## TODO: Screenshot — Create notebook

Capture: New notebook dialog / template selection.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## TODO: Screenshot — Notebook editor

Capture: Toolbar + cells (Python, SQL, Markdown) with outputs visible.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## TODO: Screenshot — Compute context

Capture: **Connected → Edit** (or equivalent) showing role, warehouse, database, schema, and EAI toggle.
:::

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
## TODO: Screenshot — Session controls

Capture: **Start / Active** session button with restart/end options.
:::

## Notebook toolbar & session {#sec-toolbar}

Workspace session controls ([reference](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-snowsight/notebooks-in-workspaces/notebooks-in-workspaces-edit-run)):

| Control | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **Start / Active** | Start kernel session; view memory/CPU; restart or end |
| **Run all / Stop** | Execute all cells or cancel |
| **Package selector** | Install Python packages (Anaconda channel on some runtimes) |
| **Scheduler** | Run notebook on a schedule via **Tasks** |
| **Connected / Edit** | Role, warehouse, database, schema, **External Access** |
| **Export / duplicate** | Share or fork notebooks |

For R: Python bootstrap cell runs first; R packages install via micromamba + YAML tarballs — not the Anaconda package picker alone.

## Cell types {#sec-cells}

| Cell type | Kernel | Use | R in this guide |
|-----------|--------|-----|-----------------|
| **Python** | Python (Jupyter/IPython) | Snowpark, pandas, `setup_notebook()` | **Required first** — installs R runtime and `%%R` magic |
| **SQL** | Warehouse | Queries, DDL, context | Same as any SQL worksheet |
| **Markdown** | — | Documentation | Explain steps to collaborators |
| **R (`%%R`)** | **Python** (rpy2 interprets cell as R) | Statistical modeling | After bootstrap — see [Architecture](../02_architecture/index.qmd) |

::: {.callout-note}
## There is no separate R kernel

An `%%R` cell is still a **Python cell**. The first line `%%R` tells IPython to hand the rest of the cell to **rpy2**, which runs it in an embedded R interpreter inside the Python process. Workspace shows it as an "R cell" in the UI, but the underlying kernel is always Python.
:::

**Execution model:**

- **SQL cells** run on the **warehouse** you selected in context, or switch to via `USE WAREHOUSE`. The warehouse can be changed at any time, and the context will be updated accordingly. This is useful for running SQL queries in different warehouse sizes.
- **Python kernel** runs on **ML Container Runtime** (compute pool selected for the notebook session)
- **R cells** execute **inside the Python process** via rpy2 — not a separate R kernel. Context (R Environment) is maintained across cell execution.

See [R Cells & Interop](../08_r_cells_and_interop/index.qmd) for data exchange patterns. Container internals (nbctl, uvenv kernel, `/filesystem`): [Appendix G](../appendices/G_workspace_container_internals/index.qmd).

## Compute setup {#sec-compute}

Notebooks use **ML Container Runtime** for the interactive kernel ([compute setup docs](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/user-guide/ui-snowsight/notebooks-in-workspaces/notebooks-in-workspaces-compute-setup)):

```{mermaid}
flowchart LR
  subgraph notebook [Notebook session]
    PY[Python kernel on compute pool]
    Rcell["R via rpy2 in same process"]
  end
  WH[Virtual warehouse]
  PY --> Rcell
  PY -->|SQL / Snowpark| WH
  SQLcell[SQL cells] --> WH
```

| Component | Runs on | Pays |
|-----------|---------|------|
| Python kernel (+ R via rpy2) | Compute pool / container runtime | Container credits |
| SQL & Snowpark queries | Virtual warehouse | Warehouse credits |
| Model training in R that calls Snowflake ML | Mixed — ML API may use warehouse + stages | Both |

**Practical tips:**

- Size warehouse for SQL-heavy notebooks; container pool for ML package install time
- **Suspend** warehouse when idle; **end session** when done with notebook
- Enable **EAI** before bootstrap if installing from CRAN/GitHub/pip ([Network & EAI](../07_network_and_eai/index.qmd))

## What Workspace provides for R teams {#sec-value}

- **Data locality** — Query and train without bulk export
- **Git-backed projects** — Reproducible notebooks and configs
- **Shared ML objects** — Same Feature Store / Registry as Python colleagues
- **Governance** — Roles, audit, network rules, tags
- **Scheduling** — Promote notebook to recurring Task

**Trade-offs vs desktop RStudio:**

- Cold-start bootstrap and EAI setup
- Notebook UX vs full IDE (Connections Pane, debugger)
- Container runtime package constraints for **serving** (conda-forge) vs interactive training

## Alternatives in the same account {#sec-alternatives}

| Option | Best for |
|--------|----------|
| **Workspace Notebooks** | Mixed Python/R/SQL, team Git repos, ML exploration |
| **[Posit Workbench Native App](https://posit.co/products/enterprise/posit-workbench/)** | Full RStudio/Posit IDE in Snowflake |
| **ML Jobs** | Non-interactive batch on container runtime |
| **Local RStudio + RSnowflake** | Familiar desktop IDE, `connections.toml` auth |
| **RStudio Server in SPCS** | Custom containerized IDE (you operate the image) |

## Next steps {#sec-next}

[Workspace Bootstrap](../06_workspace_bootstrap/index.qmd) — `setup_notebook()` and YAML.

[Network & EAI](../07_network_and_eai/index.qmd) — outbound access for installs.

[R Cells & Interop](../08_r_cells_and_interop/index.qmd) — `%%R`, outputs, path discovery.
